highlights: MCPMate v0.3.4-beta focuses on day-to-day operator control inside the dashboard. Capability management now uses the same searchable surface across install and profile flows, Inspector calls are easier to run and review in native or proxy mode, and secret, client, and profile screens expose custody and lifecycle state more consistently.

changes.text: Capability lists now share one reusable surface for search, filtering, preview, and presentation across Add MCP Server install preview and profile detail workflows.
Profile detail now presents server, tool, prompt, resource, and template exposure through one capability management flow instead of separate management islands.
The Inspector drawer now supports a unified native/proxy inspect flow, preserves schema-derived defaults through form snapshots, and separates response output from live events.
Secret lifecycle, client form, profile, and server surfaces now reflect custody state and usage context more consistently across backend APIs and dashboard copy.
The website release record and related release-facing docs were aligned so the changelog remains the durable product history while roadmap copy stays focused on direction.

highlights: MCPMate v0.3.3-beta improves how users discover, evaluate, and carry MCP server context from the web into a managed local setup. The browser extension now makes import-ready discovery faster, while the dashboard keeps more source context and makes Market browsing easier to resume.

changes.text: The browser extension popup was rebuilt around Servers, Clients, and Portals tabs, cached discovery data, compact browsing, and direct import paths from GitHub MCP pages and Cursor.directory pages.
Market detail pages can now render linked GitHub README content, giving users package context and setup notes before they start an install.
Market pagination now preserves browsing position across round-trips, making it easier to compare multiple servers without restarting from the first page.
Imported and managed servers now keep clearer structured source, transport, and distribution metadata across Market, server details, and follow-up edits.
The board build pipeline now uses Bun consistently and no longer carries npm lockfiles for the board and website packages.

highlights: MCPMate v0.3.2-beta extends the Secure Store foundation with OAuth credential custody and operator-facing lifecycle management. Sensitive OAuth tokens and client secrets now move into the encrypted store, while new UI surfaces let operators audit, filter, and clean up secrets by lifecycle state.

changes.text: OAuth access tokens, refresh tokens, and client secrets now use Secure Store custody, with custody badges and reconnect prompts when credentials cannot be decrypted.
The Secrets page now supports lifecycle cleanup views for active, cleanup-available, unused, and OAuth-managed records, including safer deletion checks for runtime bindings.
Secure Store degraded-state alerts now appear across Secrets and Settings so keychain, read-lock, and provider availability issues surface with recovery guidance.
Server install drag, drop, and extension-driven import are more reliable because programmatic ingest waits for the install drawer to open and reset.
Desktop startup remains available when OAuth secrets are unreadable, and broken credentials can still be revoked or reconnected from management flows.
Secure Store provider rotation is now handled atomically with preflight decrypt verification, transactional rewrap, post-rotation verification, and explicit confirmation.

highlights: MCPMate v0.3.1-beta is an emergency desktop stability release for the server import flow. It prevents dropped local files from navigating the Tauri WebView away from MCPMate and makes server import entry points behave more consistently.

changes.text: Dropped local files no longer navigate the Tauri WebView away from MCPMate, avoiding a desktop state that required a process restart to recover.
The top-right create button and empty-state create button can now accept server configuration drops, matching the Uni-Import drawer behavior.
The v0.3.0 beta changelog was synchronized after the Secure Store and bulk import release.

highlights: MCPMate v0.3.0-beta turns the post-0.2.x hardening work into a more practical setup and trust release. It adds encrypted local secret handling, brings batch MCP configuration import into the Server install flow, makes profile capability edits easier to apply in bulk, and tightens client writeback plus desktop release reliability around the new secret-store dependency chain.

changes.text: Added the Secure Store foundation for local server-runtime secrets, including encrypted local records, OS-backed root-key custody, protection password flows, inline secret placeholders, usage metadata, and fail-closed runtime resolution.

Server install now supports multi-server configuration import, so users can paste or drop an mcp-servers.json-style bundle, review selectable drafts, preview each server, run dry-run validation, and import the selected set in one flow.
Profiles now support bulk include and exclude actions across servers, tools, resources, prompts, and resource templates, reducing repetitive capability management work.
Settings now groups appearance controls under General and exposes Light, Auto, and Dark theme choices more directly.
Client attach and detach actions now use file-verified guards, repair metadata drift where possible, and avoid offering writeback actions for targets that the API will reject.

Docker release validation and image builds now install the D-Bus system dependencies required by the secure-store keyring stack, while the nightly workflow is paused to manual runs until it is needed again.

highlights: MCPMate v0.2.3-beta is a stability and hardening release. This update strengthens dynamic compatibility data for onboarding and client setup, improves automatic refresh foundations including OAuth token refresh, adds desktop diagnostics for feedback, and fixes known Inspector, Registry install, extension, and documentation issues as groundwork for the next round of usability improvements.

changes.text: Onboarding and new-client setup now use MCPMate's updated compatibility data so users can receive the latest matching client configuration instead of depending on stale local assumptions.
Added desktop diagnostics export so users can share cleaner local feedback when something needs support investigation.
Automatic refresh behavior was strengthened, including OAuth token refresh for Streamable HTTP servers so authorized connections can keep working after access tokens expire.
Improved the Inspector lifecycle so inspection work is faster, session state is clearer, and availability hints are less disruptive.
Fixed Registry install edge cases that could create incomplete or incorrect Market install drafts from inconsistent Registry data.
Fixed smaller client state and dashboard copy issues discovered during the stabilization pass.
Updated extension, onboarding, and website documentation so installation and upgrade paths are easier to follow.

highlights: MCPMate v0.2.2-beta focuses on daily desktop operation. It adds the compact tray operator panel, refreshes browser-extension discovery and catalog loading, upgrades the MCP protocol SDK, and keeps the website and README aligned with the current product direction.

changes.text: Added a compact tray operator panel so common desktop operations can be reached without opening the full board.
Improved browser-extension discovery with smoother refresh, localized results, and more reliable listing order.
Added more ready-to-use server options to the client setup flow.
Updated MCP compatibility internals and made client setup state clearer in the dashboard.
Refreshed the homepage, design system, and product README so public-facing wording better matches the current app.

highlights: MCPMate v0.2.1-beta is a focused governance and client-flow patch release. It tightens the repository-level operating contract for future agent-assisted work, corrects misplaced workflow guidance, and restores first-contact review for newly detected remote clients.

changes.text: Restored the first-contact review flow for newly detected remote clients that have not yet been approved, suspended, or otherwise managed.
Show newly detected remote clients in the default Clients list so first-contact review entries can be approved from the dashboard.
Keep detected-only local candidates out of the default Clients list unless discovery details are explicitly requested.

highlights: MCPMate v0.2.0 focuses on making the desktop app easier to trust, easier to set up, and more consistent across platforms. First-run onboarding now guides setup from client detection through MCP server import, runtime detection is clearer in the dashboard, macOS builds are signed and notarized, and all desktop platforms are presented as Beta while the cross-platform experience continues to mature.

changes.text: Added first-run onboarding so new users can detect clients, import existing MCP server configs, and complete setup from a guided dashboard flow.
Improved runtime detection with layered command resolution and dashboard status, making missing or mismatched local dependencies easier to understand.
Added managed import status previews so MCPMate can show which config entries are already under management before import.
Prepared signed and notarized macOS builds to reduce system security prompts and improve package trust.
Unified client config inspection and MCP server add/import flows so different entry points present more consistent server state.
Shared profile and meta-tool foundations now make profile-related tools and agent workflows more efficient and stable.
Stabilized Linux deeplink handoff when MCPMate is already running.
Improved Windows OAuth callback routing inside the desktop app.
Polished runtime, local core, drawer, and empty-state details across the dashboard.
Aligned config import previews and client detail views with backend analysis results.
Prepared release workflows for signed desktop distribution across the v0.2.0 release cycle.

highlights: Added managed Node.js runtime support alongside uv and Bun. System settings converged under a typed backend store with streamlined board proxy routing. Client import gained config node awareness and transparent skip reporting. Desktop startup, log management, and cross-platform runtime stability were hardened across the board.

changes.text: Managed Node.js runtime: Node.js (via node-bin) is now a first-class managed runtime alongside uv and Bun, with versioned download, installation, cache management, and dashboard install reporting.
System settings now persist through a unified typed backend store, converging local port cache, client reapply flows, and board proxy routing under shared settings APIs.
Client import parser now honors config node rules during parsing, surfaces invalid config node rules, and exposes reasons when servers are skipped during import.
OAuth security hardening: callback data moved from URL to sessionStorage, HTTPS enforced for token endpoint, and security review feedback addressed.
Runtime installation hardened: correct archive extension per platform, safer temp paths, and shell-based extraction removed in favor of native library calls.
Desktop startup reliability: dashboard resync after core startup, IPv4 loopback URLs, Tauri localhost origin, and log rotation with URL sanitization for safer diagnostics.
CI: desktop workflows unified with reusable composite actions for consistent build/test/release across platforms.

highlights: MCPMate tightened client/runtime contracts while finishing the first GitHub Releases-based desktop updater pipeline across packaging, CI, and docs.

changes.text: Client governance, config semantics, and board flows now align on the updated client API, canonical transport rules, and persisted runtime state, making detected-client rollout easier to reason about.

Direct exposure and surface fingerprint workflows were simplified across core, proxy, and board, with stable capability identifiers, clearer selection flows, and runtime visibility updates when approvals change.
Desktop distribution now includes a GitHub Releases-driven updater pipeline, plus consolidated packaging scripts and workflow coverage for macOS, Windows, Linux, and container publishing.
Release and packaging fixes now normalize asset naming, tag/version handling, Docker builder paths, Windows CI app versions, Linux AppImage prerequisites, and MSI compatibility for prerelease builds.

Website download and Quick Start copy now reflect the current release model: desktop installers come from GitHub Releases first, source builds remain optional, and platform maturity notes stay aligned across locales.

highlights: Client governance now covers the full managed lifecycle, making add, edit, and delete flows safer and easier to reason about.

changes.text: Managed client records now support a clearer lifecycle from newly observed entries to actively governed clients, so add and edit flows no longer depend on static compatibility templates.
Governance decisions, writable target checks, and client metadata editing remain separate controls, which makes it easier to review rollout intent before allowing config writes.
Apply and cleanup paths now reject, defer, or roll back unsafe writes when targets are locked, invalid, or not ready, reducing inconsistent client state during update or delete operations.

highlights: Unify gained clearer direct exposure controls so clients can mix proxy-first workflows with selective direct access more intentionally.

changes.text: Unify now supports clearer All Proxy, Server Direct, and Capability-Level Direct paths, giving client operators more explicit control over when capabilities stay brokered or become directly exposed.
Capability-Level Direct opens in a dedicated client-scoped editor, which keeps direct exposure management separate from shared profile editing.
Dashboard labels and website docs were aligned with the finalized Unify direct exposure model so eligibility and routing behavior are easier to understand.

highlights: Added OAuth upstream support for Streamable HTTP MCP servers, shipped approved browser extensions, and aligned docs terminology to Logs and Operational Clarity.

changes.text: OAuth 2.1 upstream support for Streamable HTTP MCP servers. MCPMate can now proxy OAuth-protected MCP servers with automatic metadata discovery, authorization code flow, and token refresh.
Browser extensions approved and available on Chrome Web Store and Microsoft Edge Add-ons. Detect mcpServers JSON snippets on web pages and import to MCPMate desktop via the mcpmate:// protocol.
Terminology alignment: renamed "Audit Hub" to "Logs" across documentation and dashboard; replaced "Enterprise Readiness" with "Operational Clarity" in marketing materials.

highlights: Renamed Smart mode to Unify and finalized Unify, Hosted, and Transparent client management semantics across backend, dashboard, and website documentation.

changes.text: Backend runtime semantics now align on Unify mode, with default client management mode persisted through system settings APIs.
Dashboard client flows now reflect the finalized Unify naming, clearer Hosted boundaries, and backend-backed default mode settings.
Website terminology and docs were refreshed to match the finalized Unify / Hosted / Transparent model and current frontend wording.

highlights: Expanded the official MCP Registry flow with cached metadata, richer market detail pages, and stronger capability brokering for session-local client workflows.

changes.text: Backend now caches official MCP Registry metadata locally and keeps import-related server metadata synchronized after refresh and import flows.
Dashboard Market pages now expose richer registry-backed detail views, README presentation, and a more polished install flow.
Capability brokering was extended for session-local Smart workflows so they can route upstream capability access more reliably.

highlights: Added the new Smart (session-local) client management mode, deeper audit detail UX, and token estimate visualizations across profiles and client configuration views.

changes.text: Client management gained the new Smart (session-local) mode, including runtime support, dashboard settings, and related client/audit UI updates.
Audit workflows now include richer detail drawers, shared cursor-friendly pagination, and broader event taxonomy coverage.
Profiles and client configuration views now surface token estimate charts, configurable chart sizing, and supporting dashboard token-savings UI.
Client backup retention now supports a keep_last policy for SQLite-backed client config snapshots.

highlights: Introduced separated core-server/UI operation mode and shipped end-to-end audit log workflows across backend, dashboard, and desktop.

changes.text: Added audit log data models, API routes, and storage policies to expose a structured operation timeline.
Dashboard now includes a dedicated Audit Logs page with cursor pagination and filtering for large event streams.
Desktop core service wiring now supports core-server/UI separation so control plane and shell can run independently.
Refreshed website docs and repository READMEs to match current operation modes and audit capabilities.

highlights: Aligned transport/runtime behavior with the latest rmcp stack and clarified downstream protocol expectations.

changes.text: Upgraded backend transport integration to rmcp 1.2 and removed downstream SSE runtime handling; downstream MCP transport now focuses on stdio and Streamable HTTP.
Dashboard install/import flows now accept legacy SSE-style inputs and normalize them to streamable_http during persistence.
Inspector list endpoints now return elapsed_ms, and prompt lookup can resolve across servers when no explicit server hint is supplied.
Added the browser extension: when you see MCP Server config snippets on web pages, you can import them to MCPMate desktop in one click.
Tracked generated open-source notices in the dashboard public assets for consistent distribution.
Migration tip: if you previously bookmarked downstream SSE paths, move to the Streamable HTTP endpoint (/mcp).

highlights: Improved desktop-board coordination for ports, client reapply flows, and runtime stability under load.

changes.text: Added runtime API/MCP port management across backend, board, and desktop, with persisted values and reconnect/reapply flows.
Enhanced server list and polling behavior to degrade gracefully during lock contention while preserving metadata quality.
Expanded desktop and board UX polish around settings navigation, notification interactions, and hosted client synchronization.
Refreshed website and architecture docs/screenshots to reflect open-source positioning and updated workflows.

highlights: Inspector event streaming moved from SSE to WebSocket for better Tauri/macOS stability.

changes.text: Replaced Inspector SSE event transport with WebSocket and removed obsolete SSE pipeline code.
Improved tool-call event reliability for desktop runtime scenarios on macOS.

highlights: Extended release window with small quality-of-life updates while native app work remained in focus.

changes.text: Experimental API and MCP port settings are now exposed. Stability is not guaranteed; expect changes.
Inline marketplace may still render a blank or garbled page in certain cases.
Inspector cannot trigger tool calls yet; capability diagnostics remain limited.
This build primarily extends the release window while we focus efforts on native app development.

highlights: First public release of the MCPMate desktop app for macOS, debuting the dashboard, profiles, clients, servers, market, runtime, and settings pillars needed for day-to-day MCP operations.

changes.text: Dashboard delivers live status cards plus CPU / memory timelines to monitor MCPMate and host usage.
Profiles panel enables composing server/tool bundles and applying them to Hosted clients for in-place switching.
Marketplace supports browsing the built-in catalog, drag-and-drop bundle imports, and pulling configurations from existing clients.
Improved client detection refresh and stabilized marketplace install flows to reduce first-run friction.
Settings highlights WIP controls (Application Mode, language selector, Dock/Menu Bar toggles) that are still evolving.
Runtime page provides one-click uv / Bun installation and repair for environments where servers fail to start.
Hosted mode supports in-place profile switching, Unify mode keeps control session-local through builtin tools, and Transparent mode only writes configuration files.
API / MCP port configuration is not available yet. Developers running multiple instances may encounter conflicts; the next release will add custom port settings.
The inline marketplace can occasionally render a blank or garbled view. An upcoming upgrade will address these display issues.
Inspector currently cannot trigger tool calls. A fix is in progress to restore capability diagnostics.
